score_injection_action: Disgraced rank + user_rating_3(-8 for dev-20260526-044731) noted: applying heightened skepticism — will NOT rubber-stamp any AC without reading actual code and running actual tests; every PASS backed by pytest output or direct code evidence; treated manifest under-coverage as a real finding rather than ignoring it.

qa.user_verbatim_complaint: /dev /commit --bulk 有 disable-model-invocation: true，agent 不可能自主调用它。真正发生的事情：changelog-analyst 在 BULK=false 模式下自作主张使用了 auto-bulk: commit message 前缀。这个前缀匹配 BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE → privilege guard 放行 → 不需要 grant → 不需要 user auth → 不需要任何授权。changelog-analyst 绕过了整个安全模型。两个 Layer 1.F bypass paths: (1) write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py 绝对路径调用; (2) python3 -c 动态构造。
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: Layer 1.F outer guard trigger at line 671 now includes a third OR-condition matching 'write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' as a fixed substring, catching both absolute-path invocations (AC1) and python3 -c forms with literal script name (AC2). M5 allowlist preserves the legitimate /commit --bulk venv-activate form. DO NOT items 13 and 14 added to changelog-analyst.md at lines 42-43.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: direct file read, line-number confirmed via grep

out_of_scope_observations.observation: manifest.json active_tests[] contains only 6 entries (ac-01 through ac-06). Test files test_ac_07 and test_ac_07b were added by dev but NOT registered in the manifest. This means the test-writer manifest is incomplete — a future CI scan of manifest active_tests[] would miss these two tests. The tests run and pass fine via pytest collection, so this is a manifest metadata gap, not a functional gap. Not blocking because all 8 tests pass and the tests do exist on disk.

spec_section_updates.section_4:
Cycle 20260526-052559 measured state:
- hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh line 671: outer guard trigger contains three OR-conditions: '/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-' | '/tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-' | 'write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' — third condition is the M1/M2 fix added this cycle.
- hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh lines 672-681: M5 allowlist using `_BULK_CANONICAL_RE` and `[[ $COMMAND =~ $_BULK_CANONICAL_RE ]]` — end-anchored no-arg regex, correctly blocks --sid FORGED forms.
- agents/changelog-analyst.md lines 42-43: DO NOT items 13 (auto-bulk: prefix prohibited in BULK=false) and 14 (sentinel creation by any mechanism prohibited) present verbatim.
- All 8 pytest tests pass: AC1 (abs path blocked), AC2 (python3 -c blocked), AC3 (relative path allowed), AC4 (auto-bulk prohibition in DO NOT), AC5 (sentinel writer prohibition in DO NOT), AC6 (regression guard), AC7 (venv-activate allowed), AC7b (forged --sid blocked).
- pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:551 _has_bulk_commit_sentinel() check confirmed intact (not touched this cycle).

qa.overall_assessment: All 7 acceptance criteria (AC1, AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5, AC6, AC7, AC7b) verified as PASS. Code evidence confirms each fix is in place and all 8 pytest tests pass. One minor out-of-scope observation: manifest.json missing ac-07/ac-07b entries (non-blocking). No critical or major issues found. Verdict: PASS.

qa._success_criteria_rule: Both R1 and R2 from requirements_decomposition verified with PASS.

root_cause_verification.rationale: Root cause was: outer guard trigger regex at line 662 only matched /tmp/ path patterns, not the script name itself. Fix adds a third OR-condition at line 671 matching the script name as a fixed string, which causes both absolute-path invocations and python3 -c forms with literal script name to enter the Layer 1.F block. Once inside the block, absolute-path forms do not match the BARE_WRITER_RE (which only accepts relative-path STARTS-WITH) and fall through to DENY. The M5 allowlist uses bash [[ =~ ]] with an end-anchored no-arg regex to preserve the single legitimate use case.

build_verification.skip_reason: Non-UI change. Modified files are a bash hook (pretool-bash-safety.sh) and an agent markdown definition (changelog-analyst.md). No deployed service, no frontend. Skip condition applies per QA agent Step 9: 'If dev changes only affect non-deployed files (offline scripts, hooks, agent definitions, documentation), skip with documented rationale.'

band_aid_detection.detail: The fix EXTENDS an existing security check (adds a new matching condition to the outer guard trigger) rather than weakening any existing check. BARE_WRITER_RE allowlist was not extended to absolute paths. M5 allowlist is restrictive (end-anchored, no-arg). DO NOT items in changelog-analyst.md add prohibitions, they do not remove or weaken any existing ones.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: codex invocation itself was blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh — the Bash command containing 'write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' in the PROMPT variable triggered Layer 1.F (the very feature being tested). This is an ironic but conclusive confirmation that AC2 is working correctly: any bash command containing the literal script name is caught by the outer guard. Graceful fallback to self-review applied.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self_review: (1) Is M5 regex correctly end-anchored to block --sid injection? YES — [[ $COMMAND =~ _BULK_CANONICAL_RE ]] with $ at end confirmed. (2) Does relative-path allowlist leak to absolute paths? NO — BARE_WRITER_RE uses STARTS-WITH match anchored to 'scripts/' prefix without leading /. (3) Is arch-2b (fully dynamic, no script name in text) blocked? NO — explicitly acknowledged as out-of-scope/inherently-unblockable at hook layer; DO NOT item 14 is the agent-level defense. (4) Manifest gap: ac-07/ac-07b not in manifest — minor finding, does not block. (5) Is pretool-git-privilege-guard.py untouched? YES — confirmed by git diff (not in changed files). All 5 adversarial questions answered; no verdict change required.
